concerns.text: Task T011 (simulate_EEG) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but explicitly depends on T010 (preprocess_dMRI) for adjacency matrices. The text notes 'T011 is NOT parallel-safe' in the dependency notes, yet the task header retains the [P] tag. This creates a semantic contradiction: a task cannot be parallel-safe if it consumes a specific artifact from a preceding task in the same phase.

Task T014 (metrics) and T015 (avalanches) are marked [P] and grouped in Phase 4. However, T015 depends on T011 (simulated EEG) and T014 depends on T010 (structural matrices). Since T010 and T011 are in Phase 3, T014/T015 cannot start until Phase 3 completes. While the 'Dependencies' section notes this, the [P] tag on T014/T015 implies they can run in parallel with *each other* (which is true) but the phase grouping suggests they are independent of Phase 3, which is false. The [P] tag is misleading without explicit 'After Phase 3' context in the task header.

Tasks T030-T035 (Revision) are listed in Phase 7. The dependency notes state they 'MUST run after the initial implementation of the respective user stories'. However, the phase ordering places them *after* Phase 6 (Polish). If T029 (end-to-end validation) runs in Phase 6, it will fail if T030-T035 (which fix critical data source and failure logic) are not yet implemented. The Revision phase must precede the final validation task (T029) in Phase 6, or T029 must be moved to Phase 8.

Task T012b is listed after T012. While T012b depends on T012 (reporting logic depends on QC checks), the sequential numbering (12, 12b) is clear. However, T012b is marked [US1] but lacks a [P] tag, implying it must run after T012. This is correct, but the visual grouping in Phase 3 suggests all tasks can start after Phase 2. A clearer dependency arrow or 'After T012' note in the description would prevent ambiguity.
